senses.definition: a state of low priority where something is set aside to be dealt with later

examples.x: Due to the budget constraints, we had to put the new office expansion project on the back burner.
I had to put my dream of traveling around the world on the back burner while I focused on my career.
With the urgent deadlines for the upcoming conference, our marketing campaign has been moved to the back burner.
His personal goals have been on the back burner since he became the primary caregiver for his ailing father.
The idea was on the back burner for now, but I’ll revisit it later.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv bˈɪɡɚ bˈɛɾɚɹ ˈʌðɚ fˈɪʃ tʊ fɹˈaɪ kˈætʃ

senses.definition: to have more interesting or important matters to deal with

examples.x: I don't have time for your trivial complaints; I have bigger fish to fry at work.
I can't help you with your computer problems right now; I have better fish to catch.
I appreciate your invitation, but I have other fish to fry this weekend, and I won't be able to join you.
Dealing with this minor customer request is not a priority; we have better fish to catch, such as resolving a major service outage.

senses.definition: to begin talking about basic or important facts of a situation

examples.x: The situation was so serious that after a few polite exchanges they quickly got down to brass tacks.
Let's get down to brass tacks and discuss how we're going to solve this problem.
I'm tired of all the hypotheticals and want to get down to brass tacks.
Once we get down to brass tacks, we'll see that there are a few different options available to us.
I'm not interested in all the theoretical discussions, I just want to get down to brass tacks.

senses.definition: used when one has to focus on the simplest and most important facts of a situation, particularly those that one was ignoring, in order to get the intended result
an individual who is not as important or influential as someone else in an organization

examples.x: The CEO is the boss, but the CFO is the second banana.
The quarterback is the star of the team, but the offensive coordinator is the second banana.
The lead singer is the most famous member of the band, but the drummer is the second banana.
The politician is the one who gives the speeches, but the campaign manager is the second banana who makes sure everything runs smoothly.
The CEO is the face of the company, but the COO is the second banana who makes sure the company runs smoothly.

senses.definition: a person who does not receive as much attention or is not as influential as someone else in a group or organization

examples.x: The CEO is the big kahuna, and everyone else is just the second fiddle.
The politician is the one who makes the decisions, and the advisors are just the second fiddles.
He's the second fiddle in our band, but he's a great musician.
She's the second fiddle to her more famous sister, but she's a talented actress in her own right.
He's always been the second fiddle to his younger brother, but he's finally starting to come into his own.

senses.definition: to be placed in a less important or weaker position compared to that of someone else

examples.x: After years of being the star player, the veteran athlete has taken a back seat to the younger players on the team.
When her husband was elected mayor, the former city councilwoman took a back seat in local politics.
With the advent of digital photography, film photography has taken a back seat in the photography industry.

senses.definition: used to state that there are more important things that one must do or consider before trying to deal with other things

examples.x: First things first, let's get the fire under control before we worry about anything else.
I know you're eager to start decorating the new house, but first things first, we need to unpack and get settled in.
I'm tired of all the distractions and want to get down to brass tacks and focus on first things first.
Once we get down to the nitty-gritty, we'll see that there are a few different options available to us, but first things first.

word_list.pronunciation: æt ðə tˈɑːp ʌvðə ɐdʒˈɛndə lˈɪst

senses.definition: in the position of utmost importance or high priority

examples.x: I like all of his books, but this one is at the top of the list.
What do you think is at the top of the agenda for today's meeting?

word_list.pronunciation: ðə lˈæst θˈɪŋ ˌɑːn wˈʌnz mˈaɪnd

senses.definition: something that is not being thought about or considered, often due to more pressing or immediate concerns

examples.x: Right now work is the last thing on my mind.
Getting married is the last thing on my mind right now.
Having a pet is the last thing on my mind right now, I barely have time to take care of myself.
Quitting his job is the last thing on his mind, but he's not happy here.

senses.definition: an alternative that is not as fulfilling or desirable as other options that one has

examples.x: John aimed to win the competition, but he ended up in second place, settling for second best.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɔːl ɔːɹ dɹˈɑːp baɪ ðə wˈeɪsaɪd

senses.definition: to not have the same importance or urgency as something else

examples.x: Many aspiring musicians start their careers with great enthusiasm, but few succeed; most talented individuals unfortunately fall by the wayside.
When the company faced financial difficulties, several projects had to be discontinued, and some employees fell by the wayside as a result of downsizing.
The political campaign began with numerous candidates, but as the race progressed, several contenders dropped by the wayside due to low poll numbers or scandals.
During the pursuit of higher education, some students struggle to keep up with the demands of their studies and eventually fall by the wayside, opting to drop out of school.

senses.definition: to see something as extremely necessary or important

examples.x: A retail group which sets great store by traditional values.
Sarah has always set great store by honesty and integrity in her personal and professional relationships.
The company sets great store by customer satisfaction, striving to provide exceptional service and meet their needs.
In their culture, they set great store by family traditions, upholding them with deep reverence and respect.
John sets great store by punctuality, always arriving early for meetings and appointments.

senses.definition: a state of high priority where something is dealt with immediately or given the most attention

examples.x: The new project is now on the front burner after the meeting.
We need to move this task to the front burner before the deadline.
The proposal is now on the front burner due to recent changes.
The safety issue has been moved to the front burner after the incident.
The marketing strategy is on the front burner for the upcoming campaign.
Her career took the front burner while other plans were set aside.
